 {"id":"bd-3n00","title":"Create Unit Tests for Test Harness & Utilities","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the test harness itself, ensuring the test infrastructure is reliable before using it to test rch.

## Why This Matters
- A buggy test harness produces false positives/negatives
- Harness bugs are harder to debug than rch bugs
- Unit tests catch harness issues before they affect e2e tests
- Provides documentation of expected harness behavior

## Test Categories

### 1. Logging Infrastructure Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/logging_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_log_entry_is_valid_json() {
    let logger = TestLogger::new("test_example");
    logger.info("test message", &[("key", "value")]);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    for line in log_content.lines() {
        serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(line).expect("invalid JSON");
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_timing_accuracy() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_log_levels_filter_correctly() { ... }
```

### 2. Output Comparison Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/output_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_identical_outputs_compare_equal() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_path_normalization() {
    let local = "/home/user/project/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let remote = "/tmp/rch/project_abc/src/main.rs:10:5";
    let normalizer = OutputNormalizer::default();
    assert_eq!(normalizer.normalize(local), normalizer.normalize(remote));
}

#[test]
fn test_diff_shows_actual_differences() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_binary_comparison_exact() { ... }
```

### 3. Worker Discovery Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/discovery_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_workers_available_returns_false_when_no_config() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_skip_mechanism_logs_reason() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_health_check_timeout() { ... }
```

### 4. Harness Lifecycle Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/harness_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_daemon_spawn_and_cleanup() {
    let harness = TrueE2EHarness::new_or_skip().unwrap();
    // Daemon should be running
    assert!(harness.daemon_is_healthy());
    drop(harness);
    // Daemon should be cleaned up (if harness spawned it)
}

#[test]
fn test_fixture_isolation() { ... }

#[test]
fn test_worker_project_cleanup() { ... }
```

### 5. Timing Utilities Tests
```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/timing_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_measure_command_timing_accuracy() {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let measured = measure_command_timing("sleep 0.1");
    let actual = start.elapsed();
    // Should be within 10ms of actual
    assert!((measured.as_millis() as i64 - actual.as_millis() as i64).abs() < 10);
}

#[test]
fn test_percentile_calculation() { ... }
```

## Test Organization
```
tests/
└── true_e2e/
    ├── mod.rs
    ├── harness.rs
    ├── logging.rs
    ├── output.rs
    └── tests/           # Unit tests for harness
        ├── mod.rs
        ├── logging_tests.rs
        ├── output_tests.rs
        ├── discovery_tests.rs
        ├── harness_tests.rs
        └── timing_tests.rs
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% code coverage for logging module
- [ ] All output comparison edge cases tested
- [ ] Worker discovery tests run without real workers
- [ ] Harness lifecycle tested end-to-end
- [ ] All tests pass with `cargo test` (no workers needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:32:54.582781639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.357344236Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:55:42.356337229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:18.952614737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-1tq2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:16.906775866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3r2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:17.872745681Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3n00","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:15.663722245Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3n1d","title":"Add TUI horizontal scroll for long text content","description":"# Add Horizontal Scroll for Long Text

## Problem
Long worker IDs, hostnames, or build commands get truncated in TUI panels. Users can't see full content.

## Examples
```
┌─ Workers ─────────────────────────────┐
│ ● production-us-east-1-buil...  (4/8) │  ← Truncated\!
│ ● production-us-west-2-buil...  (2/8) │
│ ● staging-worker-with-very-...  (0/8) │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Proposed Solution

### Option 1: Scroll on Selection
When a row is selected, show full content in a status bar:
```
┌─ Workers ─────────────────────────────┐
│ ● production-us-east-1-build-srv (4/8)│
│ ● production-us-west-2-buil...  (2/8) │
├───────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Selected: production-us-east-1-build-server.internal.company.com │
└───────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Option 2: Horizontal Scroll Keys
Left/Right arrows scroll the content:
```
← h/→ l: scroll | ↑ k/↓ j: select
...duction-us-east-1-build-server.int...
```

### Recommendation
Implement Option 1 (status bar) first - simpler and more discoverable.

## Implementation
1. Add detail_line field to TuiState for selected item info
2. When selection changes, update detail_line
3. Render detail_line in a status bar below the panel

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/tui/state.rs (add detail_line field)
- rch/src/tui/widgets.rs (render detail bar)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:13:44.221313199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:13:44.221313199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3nbh","title":"Implement fleet update for rch update (deploy rch-wkr to workers)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:00:16.452131082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:04:13.911704076Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:04:13.911607335Z","close_reason":"Implemented fleet update via workers deploy-binary","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3ncy","title":"Add confirmation prompts to destructive CLI commands","description":"# Add Confirmation Prompts to Destructive Commands

## Problem
Several commands take immediate action without confirmation, which can disrupt active work:

### rch daemon restart (main.rs line 678)
- Restarts daemon immediately
- Interrupts all active SSH connections
- Can cause in-progress builds to fail
- No warning, no confirmation

### rch workers drain
- Immediately stops routing to worker
- No confirmation for single worker
- --all has --yes flag but single worker doesn't

## Commands Needing Confirmation

| Command | Risk | Current | Proposed |
|---------|------|---------|----------|
| rch daemon restart | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch daemon stop | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch workers drain | Stops routing | None (single) | Always confirm |
| rch fleet drain --all | Stops all routing | --yes flag | Keep as-is |
| rch cancel --all | Kills all builds | --yes flag | Keep as-is |

## Implementation Pattern
```rust
async fn daemon_restart(force: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let status = get_daemon_status().await?;
    
    if \!force && status.active_jobs > 0 {
        eprintln\!("Warning: {} active jobs will be interrupted.", status.active_jobs);
        if \!confirm("Restart daemon?")? {
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    
    // Proceed with restart
}
```

## Bypass for Automation
All confirmation prompts should respect:
- --yes / -y flag to skip confirmation
- Non-interactive mode (no TTY) should require --yes

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/commands.rs (add confirmation logic)
- rch/src/main.rs (add --yes flag to restart/stop if missing)

## Testing
- Verify prompts appear for destructive actions
- Verify --yes bypasses prompts
- Verify non-TTY fails without --yes
- Verify JSON output mode works correctly","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:45.231015279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:29:07.244582690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","depends_on_id":"bd-2u2g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:43:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (rch/src/commands_test.rs)
1. `test_daemon_restart_prompts_when_active_builds()`
2. `test_daemon_restart_skips_prompt_with_yes_flag()`
3. `test_daemon_restart_skips_prompt_when_no_active()`
4. `test_workers_drain_prompts_for_confirmation()`
5. `test_confirmation_denied_aborts_action()`
6. `test_non_tty_requires_yes_flag()`

### Integration Tests
1. Start active build, try restart, verify prompt appears
2. Run with --yes, verify no prompt
3. Run without TTY, verify error without --yes

### E2E Test Script (scripts/test_confirmations.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE=${RCH_TEST_LOG:-/tmp/rch_confirm_test.log}

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

# Test 1: daemon restart prompts
log "Test 1: daemon restart should prompt with active builds"
# Start a slow build
rch self-test --worker css &
BUILD_PID=$!
sleep 2

# Try restart without --yes, should prompt (and fail in non-TTY)
if echo "n" | rch daemon restart 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart"; then
  log "PASS: Restart prompts for confirmation"
else
  log "WARN: Could not verify prompt (may be non-TTY mode)"
fi

kill $BUILD_PID 2>/dev/null || true

# Test 2: --yes bypasses prompt
log "Test 2: --yes bypasses confirmation"
rch daemon restart --yes 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart" || {
  log "PASS: --yes bypassed confirmation"
}

# Test 3: Non-TTY requires --yes
log "Test 3: Non-TTY without --yes should fail"
if echo "" | rch daemon restart 2>&1 | grep -qi "requires.*--yes"; then
  log "PASS: Non-TTY correctly requires --yes"
fi

log "Confirmation tests complete"
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log confirmation prompt decision at INFO level
- Log user response at DEBUG level
- Log bypass via --yes at DEBUG level","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:15Z"}]}

## Problem
Several commands take immediate action without confirmation, which can disrupt active work:

- Can cause in-progress builds to fail
- No warning, no confirmation

- Immediately stops routing to worker

- --all has --yes flag but single worker doesn't

| Command | Risk | Current | Proposed |
|---------|------|---------|----------|
| rch daemon restart | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch daemon stop | Interrupts builds | None | Confirm if active builds |
| rch workers drain | Stops routing | None (single) | Always confirm |
| rch fleet drain --all | Stops all routing | --yes flag | Keep as-is |
| rch cancel --all | Kills all builds | --yes flag | Keep as-is |

## Implementation Pattern
```rust
async fn daemon_restart(force: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let status = get_daemon_status().await?;
    
    if \!force && status.active_jobs > 0 {
        eprintln\!("Warning: {} active jobs will be interrupted.", status.active_jobs);
        if \!confirm("Restart daemon?")? {
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    
    // Proceed with restart
}
```

## Bypass for Automation
All confirmation prompts should respect:
- --yes / -y flag to skip confirmation
- Non-interactive mode (no TTY) should require --yes

## Files to Modify
- rch/src/commands.rs (add confirmation logic)
- rch/src/main.rs (add --yes flag to restart/stop if missing)

## Testing

- Verify non-TTY fails without --yes
- Verify JSON output mode works correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:45.231015279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:10:13.977251596Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:10:13.976768647Z","close_reason":"Implemented confirmation prompts for daemon stop/restart in commit 148efa4","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","depends_on_id":"bd-2u2g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:43:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-3ncy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

# Test 1: daemon restart prompts

BUILD_PID=$!
sleep 2

# Try restart without --yes, should prompt (and fail in non-TTY)
if echo "n" | rch daemon restart 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart"; then
  log "PASS: Restart prompts for confirmation"
else
  log "WARN: Could not verify prompt (may be non-TTY mode)"
fi

kill $BUILD_PID 2>/dev/null || true

# Test 2: --yes bypasses prompt
log "Test 2: --yes bypasses confirmation"
rch daemon restart --yes 2>&1 | grep -qi "restart" || {
  log "PASS: --yes bypassed confirmation"

# Test 3: Non-TTY requires --yes
log "Test 3: Non-TTY without --yes should fail"

log "Confirmation tests complete"
```

- Log confirmation prompt decision at INFO level

 {"id":"bd-3ndq","title":"Implement JobLifecycleLog for structured job event display","description":"Create JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs for job event visualization:
- Job submission: job ID, source, command summary, assigned worker
- Job progress: compilation phase, elapsed time, resource usage
- Job completion: success/failure indicator, duration, artifact count
- Job failure: error type, worker state, suggested remediation

Technical requirements:
- Prefix each line with timestamp and job ID for log grep-ability
- Use consistent column alignment for scanability
- Color code by event type: blue (start), yellow (progress), green (success), red (failure)
- Include Unicode status icons inline
- Rate-limit progress updates (max 1/second) to prevent log spam
- Support both single-line compact and multi-line detailed modes

Format examples:
[14:30:01] [j-a3f2] ● START cargo build --release → worker1
[14:30:15] [j-a3f2] ◐ BUILD 14.0s │ Compiling foo v0.1.0 (45/120 crates)
[14:30:45] [j-a3f2] ✓ DONE  44.2s │ 156 artifacts │ cache written
[14:30:46] [j-b7c1] ✗ FAIL  12.1s │ worker2 timeout │ retry queued","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoReef","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:07:49.774732595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:53:59.335814886Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:53:59.335719078Z","close_reason":"Implemented JobLifecycleLog formatter in rchd/src/ui/jobs.rs (compact/detailed, colors/icons, per-job rate limiting) with unit tests; verified via cargo test -p rchd jobs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-1z6p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ndq","depends_on_id":"bd-3u68","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nxf","title":"Expand unit tests for rchd/alerts.rs (currently 2 tests)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:01:52.595913900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:51:53.205800700Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:51:53.205717575Z","close_reason":"Expanded unit tests from 2 to 29 tests.  {"id":"bd-3nzd","title":"Unit Tests: Worker Process (No Mocks)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for rch-wkr (worker) process without mock infrastructure.

## Target Files
- rch-wkr/src/executor.rs - Command execution
- rch-wkr/src/cache.rs - Build cache management

## Test Requirements
1. **Executor Tests**
   - Test command parsing and execution
   - Test timeout handling
   - Test signal propagation
   - Test output streaming
   - Test exit code capture

2. **Cache Tests**
   - Test cache key generation
   - Test cache hit/miss logic
   - Test cache eviction (LRU)
   - Test cache corruption recovery
   - Test concurrent cache access

## Constraints
- NO MockProcess or fake executors
- Use real subprocess execution (simple commands)
- Use real file system for cache
- Test actual cache behavior

## Logging Requirements
- Each test: TEST START/TEST PASS format
- Log command and exit codes
- Include cache state on failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldGrove","created_at":"2026-01-25T22:59:48.390432852Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:48:18.008402654Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:48:18.008336942Z","close_reason":"Worker process unit tests done; cache cleanup tests isolated via cleanup_in; gates pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nzd","depends_on_id":"bd-1aim","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
